title: 10 best herbal creams for cramps

remedy_properties: Vasodilation: Guelder rose cream contains vasodilators like berberine, which help to relax blood vessels and improve circulation, reducing spasms and alleviating cramping pain.

Antispasmodic effects: The active compounds in guelder rose, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, have antispasmodic properties that help to block nerve impulses responsible for muscle contractions and relieve cramps.

Analgesic and anti-inflammatory activity: Guelder rose cream also contains analgesics like salicin, which helps to reduce pain perception, while its anti-inflammatory properties alleviate swelling and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Viburnum opulus, also known as guelder rose, creams helps with cramps because of its rich content of salicin and other bioactive compounds. These compounds work by relaxing the uterine muscles, reducing inflammation and alleviating spasms that cause menstrual cramps. Additionally, the creams' anti-oxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the body, providing quick relief from crampy discomfort. As a result, Viburnum opulus creams have become a popular natural remedy for menstrual cramps and associated symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Scopoletin: This coumarin derivative has a sedative effect, helping to relax muscles and reduce cramping by influencing the nervous system.
Isoferulic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce muscle spasms and alleviate cramping.
Viburnin: This alkaloid has a sedative and antispasmodic effect, helping to calm muscle contractions and ease cramping.
remedy_parts: Buds: They are used to make creams for cramps due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used to create creams for cramps because they have a spasmolytic effect, helping to relieve muscle cramps.
Fruits: The fruits of Viburnum opulus are used to make creams for cramps due to their anti-inflammatory and sedative properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the viburnum opulus leaves and flowers in late summer or early fall when they are fresh and full of fragrance.
Dry the harvested viburnum opulus material in a warm dark place for 7 to 10 days to reduce moisture content.
Combine 20 grams of dried viburnum opulus with 100 grams of carrier oil in a clean glass jar and steep for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then add 10 grams of beeswax to the oil and heat until melted.
Pour the melted mixture into small tin containers and allow to cool and solidify before use as a topical cream.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lavender's antispasmodic properties help to relax muscle contractions and reduce the frequency and severity of cramps, making it an effective natural remedy for menstrual cramps.

Anti-inflammatory properties: English lavender cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and pain associated with cramps, providing relief from discomfort and promoting overall well-being.

Calming and sedative effects: Lavender's calming and sedative properties have a soothing effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and stress that often exacerbate cramp symptoms, allowing for a more restful and comfortable recovery.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with cramps because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The active compounds in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have a calming effect on the body, reducing muscle tension and spasms that cause cramps. Additionally, lavender's antispasmodic properties help to relax the uterine muscles, providing relief from menstrual cramps and promoting a restful night's sleep, making it a popular natural remedy for cramp relief.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has been shown to have a relaxing effect on the muscles, reducing muscle spasms and cramps by inhibiting the release of neurotransmitters that stimulate muscle contractions.
Linalyl acetate: Similar to linalool, linalyl acetate is a terpene that has been found to have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce muscle tension and alleviate cramps.

Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has been shown to have a potent anti-inflammatory effect, which can help to reduce muscle inflammation and discomfort associated with cramps, while also having a relaxing effect on the muscles.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high concentration of linalool and linalyl acetate, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe cramps.
Leaves: Leaves contain similar compounds to flowers and are used for their calming effects and ability to reduce pain and inflammation associated with cramps.

Stems: Stems of Lavandula angustifolia contain a lesser amount of essential oils compared to flowers and leaves, but still contribute to the overall analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties of the creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in early morning to preserve essential oils.
Combine 1 cup of dried flowers with 2 cups of distilled water in a saucepan.
Simmer the mixture at 100-110°f for 10-15 minutes to release plant compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter with 1/4 cup of the herbal infusion.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sage cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like camphor, salvene, and borneol, which help reduce inflammation and ease muscle spasms associated with menstrual cramps.

Antispasmodic effects: Herbal sage has been traditionally used to relieve menstrual cramps due to its antispasmodic properties, which help relax smooth muscles and reduce uterine contractions that cause pain.

Pain-relieving compounds: Sage cream contains analgesic compounds like thujone and bornyl acetate, which help block the transmission of pain signals to the brain, providing relief from menstrual cramp discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia sclarea, also known as sage, creams helps with cramps because of its natural anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The essential oils present in sage, such as camphor and borneol, have a cooling effect on the skin, which can help to relax tense muscles. Additionally, sage has a sedative effect on the nervous system, reducing muscle spasms and cramping. By applying a sage cream to the affected area, you can experience relief from menstrual cramps, muscle spasms, and other types of cramping.
remedy_constituents: Terpenes: Terpenes present in Salvia sclarea creams have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help relax muscles and reduce cramp severity.
Rosmarinic acid (a phenolic compound): Rosmarinic acid in Salvia sclarea creams has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help alleviate cramp pain and reduce muscle spasms.
Camphor: Camphor in Salvia sclarea creams has a mild antispasmodic effect, helping to relax muscles and reduce cramp intensity by reducing nerve activity.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used due to their high content of salvinorin, which is believed to help alleviate menstrual cramps and other pain-related issues.
Flowers: Flowers are used due to their essential oil content, which includes sesquiterpenes that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Stems are used due to their ability to provide a concentrated source of essential oils, which are used in creams to help soothe and calm cramping muscles.
remedy_recipe: Extract 10 grams of dried salvia sclarea flowers in a glass beaker using a solvent like glycerin or ethanol.
Combine the extracted plant material with 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler to melt the wax.
Add 100 grams of coconut oil to the melted beeswax mixture and stir for 5 minutes to combine.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and add 10 grams of vitamin e oil to the mixture.
Pour the cooled mixture into a container and store it in a cool dark place for 2 weeks before use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate cramping pain by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause muscle contractions.

Antioxidant activity: Herbal ginger creams contain antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene, which help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to muscle cramps by damaging cells and tissues.

Muscle relaxant properties: Ginger's muscarinic receptors stimulation property helps to relax muscles and reduce spasms, providing relief from menstrual cramps, muscle cramps, and other types of pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with cramps because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to reduce pain and relax muscles. When applied topically, these compounds can penetrate the skin and help alleviate cramp symptoms. Additionally, the warming sensation produced by ginger creams can help increase blood flow to the affected area, further reducing cramp severity and promoting relaxation. This natural remedy offers a soothing and effective solution for menstrual cramps.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These sesquiterpene compounds have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce pain and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols are also responsible for ginger's anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can help alleviate cramping and spasms.
6-Gingerol: As a potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound, 6-gingerol helps to relax smooth muscle tissue, reducing the severity and frequency of menstrual cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are used because they contain a high amount of gingerols, which help to relax muscles and reduce cramping.
Buds: Buds are used for their high content of gingerols and other bioactive compounds that have a calming effect on the body and help to alleviate cramping.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of fresh zingiber officinale roots in late spring or early summer when the plants are mature.
Dry the harvested roots in a single layer at 50°c for 24 hours to prevent moisture retention.
Grind the dried zingiber officinale roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 5 grams of the ground zingiber officinale powder with 50 grams of a neutral base cream in a double boiler.
Allow the mixture to emulsify and cool to room temperature over 30 minutes before applying.

remedy_properties: Curcumin: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce muscle pain and inflammation associated with menstrual cramps.

Cyclooxygenase inhibition: Curcumin in turmeric cream inhibits the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like compounds that cause uterine contractions and lead to menstrual cramp pain, thereby providing relief from cramps.

Vasodilation: Turmeric's active compounds, including curcumin, have been shown to dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing constriction in the uterus, which can help alleviate menstrual cramp pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with cramps because it contains curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that reduces muscle spasms and relaxes the uterine muscles. The bioavailability of curcumin in topical creams allows it to directly target the affected area, providing quick relief from cramps. Additionally, curcuma longa creams may also help regulate menstrual cycles and reduce oxidative stress, further alleviating cramp-related discomfort and promoting overall well-being. This makes it a popular natural remedy for menstrual cramps.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin is a phenolic compound that helps with cramps by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause muscle contractions and pain.
Turmerone (Ar-turmerone): Turmerone is a terpene that helps with cramps by reducing inflammation and relaxing muscle spasms, providing relief from pain and discomfort.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): Demethoxycurcumin is a curcuminoid that helps with cramps by inhibiting the activity of COX-2 enzymes, which are involved in the production of prostaglandins and other inflammatory mediators.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Used due to their high turmeric content, which has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help alleviate cramp symptoms.
Roots: Utilized for their rich content of curcuminoids, which have potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that provide relief from cramps.
Leaves: Employed for their essential oils, which have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties that help ease cramp pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 2 tablespoons of curcuma longa powder and 2 tablespoons of beeswax.
Melt 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler.
Add 2 tablespoons of honey and 2 tablespoons of distilled water to the melted mixture.
Whip the mixture with an electric mixer for 5 minutes until it thickens.
Pour the mixture into small jars and refrigerate for 30 minutes before use.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Herbal dong quai creams can help relax uterine muscles and reduce spasms, alleviating menstrual cramp pain. This is because the active compounds in dong quai, such as ferulic acid and apiin, have a calming effect on smooth muscle tissue.

Anti-inflammatory property: Dong quai creams may help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with menstrual cramps by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in dong quai creams can help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to menstrual cramp severity and duration.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis, also known as dong quai, creams helps with cramps because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to relax muscles. The herb contains compounds like ferulic acid and ligustilide, which help to reduce muscle spasms and ease cramp-related pain. Additionally, Angelica sinensis creams may help to regulate hormonal imbalances, which can contribute to menstrual cramps. By applying these creams topically, individuals can experience relief from cramps and discomfort, making them a popular natural remedy for menstrual relief.
remedy_constituents: Fern-8-gingerol: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce muscle spasms and alleviate cramp symptoms.
Ligustilide: A non-terpenoid compound with a sedative effect, ligustilide can help relax smooth muscle and reduce muscle cramps.
Butylphthalide: A sesquiterpene lactone with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, butylphthalide can help relieve pain and reduce muscle spasms associated with cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: Angelica sinensis roots are the most commonly used part for making creams to alleviate cramps due to their high content of ferulic acid and other bioactive compounds that help relax the uterus.
Rhyzomes: Angelica sinensis rhyzomes are also widely used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce cramp pain and discomfort.
Seeds: Angelica sinensis seeds are sometimes used in combination with other parts to enhance the effectiveness of the creams due to their reported uterine relaxing properties.
remedy_recipe: Dry 500g of angelica sinensis roots in a low-temperature oven at 50c for 2 hours.
Grind 200g of dried angelica sinensis roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 200g of the ground angelica sinensis powder with 200g of beeswax and 200g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60c for 30 minutes until the beeswax and coconut oil melt.
Strain the hot mixture through a cheesecloth and bottle it for use as a herbal angelica sinensis cream.

remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover creams contain isoflavones, which have been shown to relax uterine muscles and reduce menstrual cramp severity by blocking calcium channels and reducing muscle contractions.
Vasodilating compounds: The cream's vasodilating properties help to dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing the constriction of blood vessels that can contribute to menstrual cramps.

Anti-inflammatory agents: Red clover creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and saponins, which can reduce inflammation and alleviate cramp symptoms by blocking prostaglandin production.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Trifolium pratense, also known as red clover, creams helps with cramps because of its high concentration of isoflavones, particularly formononetin and genistein. These compounds mimic the body's natural estrogen, which can help to regulate menstrual cycles and reduce the severity of cramps. The anti-inflammatory properties of red clover also help to soothe the uterine lining, reducing spasms and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps. As a result, red clover creams have become a popular natural remedy for alleviating menstrual cramp symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavones: Isoflavones, particularly genistein and daidzein, help with cramps by reducing prostaglandin production, which contributes to muscle contraction and pain.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid and sinapic acid, may help alleviate cramps by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, thereby relaxing muscle spasms.
Coumarins: Coumarins, including coumarin and esculetin, may contribute to cramp relief by modulating the activity of ion channels and receptors involved in muscle contraction and relaxation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain isoflavones, which have estrogen-like properties that help to relax the uterine muscles and alleviate menstrual cramps.
Roots: They are rich in coumarins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with cramps.
Leaves: They contain salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain caused by cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of trifolium pratense flowers and leaves by cutting them at the base of the plant with scissors.
Dry the trifolium pratense flowers and leaves in a warm place for 1-2 weeks or using a dehydrator for 6-8 hours.
Steep 1/2 cup of dried trifolium pratense in 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for 2-4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix the infused oil with a preservative such as vitamin e oil and beeswax to create a stable and moisturizing cream.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Motherwort creams have antispasmodic properties, which help to relax muscle spasms and reduce menstrual cramp severity by calming down overactive uterine contractions.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Motherwort creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and pain associated with menstrual cramps by minimizing prostaglandin production, a hormone that can cause uterine contractions.

Oxytocic-relaxant properties: Motherwort creams have oxytocic-relaxant properties, which help to counteract the stimulating effects of oxytocin, a hormone that can increase uterine contractions and worsen menstrual cramps, promoting relaxation and reducing spasms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Leonurus cardiaca, also known as motherwort, creams helps with cramps because of its natural ability to relax uterine muscles and regulate menstrual cycles. The herb contains compounds such as iridoid glycosides and flavonoids, which have a calming effect on the body. By reducing spasms and inflammation, Leonurus cardiaca creams can provide relief from menstrual cramps and associated discomfort. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe and calm the body, promoting a sense of well-being and comfort during times of cramping.
remedy_constituents: Levonurine: A phenolic compound that acts as a muscle relaxant and helps to reduce cramp symptoms by soothing muscle spasms and promoting relaxation.
Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic diterpene that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, helping to reduce muscle cramps by inhibiting muscle contractions and alleviating pain.
Levostilbene: A phenolic compound with vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to relieve cramp symptoms by improving blood flow, reducing inflammation, and relaxing muscles.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain essential oils and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties, helping to relieve cramps.
Roots: The roots of Leonurus cardiaca are rich in iridoids, which have been shown to have a sedative effect on the uterus and help alleviate menstrual cramps.
Flowers: The flowers of Leonurus cardiaca contain flavonoids and essential oils that can help to relax the uterine muscles and reduce spasms associated with menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of dried leonurus cardiaca flowers and leaves by cutting them above the node using clean scissors.
Combine the dried plant material with 500ml of carrier oil such as coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place by shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids after 2 hours.
Mix 10g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter into 50ml of the infused oil and heat the mixture gently until smooth.

remedy_properties: Agrostanols: Agrostanols, a type of saponin found in chaste tree, have been shown to relax smooth muscle and reduce spasms, providing relief from menstrual cramps.
Vasodilatory effects: Chaste tree creams contain compounds that dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing cramping by increasing oxygenation to the uterine muscle.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in chaste tree creams help neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to menstrual cramp discomfort and severity.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus castus, also known as chaste tree, creams helps with cramps because they contain the active compound aucubin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. This helps to relax the uterine muscles, reducing the severity of menstrual cramps. Additionally, the cream's hormonal balancing effects can help regulate the menstrual cycle, leading to fewer and less severe cramps. The cream may also have a sedative effect, promoting relaxation and reducing overall discomfort associated with menstrual cramps.
remedy_constituents: Agnuside: This alkaloid helps alleviate cramps by exerting a relaxing effect on smooth muscle tissues, reducing spasms and pain associated with menstrual cramps.

Iridoid glycosides (e.g., aucubin and agnuside): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties, which help reduce inflammation and relax smooth muscle tissues, providing relief from cramps.

Rosmarinic acid (a phenolic diterpene): This phenolic compound helps combat oxidative stress, inflammation, and pain associated with menstrual cramps, promoting a relaxing effect on the uterus and reducing discomfort.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high content of flavonoids, which are believed to have a relaxing effect on the uterine muscles, thereby reducing menstrual cramps.
Leaves: They are used as they contain iridoid glycosides, which are thought to have a spasmolytic effect, helping to ease menstrual cramp pain.
Seeds: They are used due to their high content of ursolic acid, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried vitex agnus castus flowers and leaves at the end of the growing season for maximum potency.
Combine 2 ounces of dried vitex agnus castus with 2 ounces of jojoba oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 2 hours at 160°f to 180°f to infuse oils.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 30 minutes of steeping time.
Blend the infused oil with 1 ounce of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil to thicken the cream.

remedy_properties: Parthenolide: A sesquiterpene lactone compound found in feverfew, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve muscle cramps by blocking calcium channels and reducing smooth muscle contraction.

Feverfew essential oils: The oils extracted from feverfew leaves contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce pain and swelling associated with cramps, as well as relax muscles to ease spasms.

Tannins: Feverfew creams often contain tannins, a type of polyphenol compound that helps to constrict blood vessels, reduce inflammation, and relax smooth muscle tissue, all of which contribute to the relief of menstrual cramps.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tanacetum parthenium, also known as feverfew, creams helps with cramps because of its unique ability to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that stimulate uterine contractions. By inhibiting the production of these prostaglandins, the creams can help alleviate menstrual cramps, also known as dysmenorrhea. Additionally, feverfew contains anti-inflammatory compounds that may help to relax the uterine muscles, further reducing cramp severity. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to provide relief from menstrual cramps, making it a popular choice for those seeking a more holistic approach to pain management.
remedy_constituents: Parthenolide: This sesquiterpene lactone has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce muscle spasms and alleviate cramp pain.
Tannins: These polyphenolic compounds have astringent and antispasmodic effects, which can help to relax muscles and reduce cramping.
Flavonoids: These plant metabolites have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help to reduce muscle spasms and alleviate cramp pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high concentration of salvene, a compound that has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: The flowers are used because they contain tannins and other compounds that can help relax muscles and reduce pain associated with cramps.
Roots: The roots are used for their ability to ease cramps and spasms due to their content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tanacetum parthenium flowers and leaves in the early morning to ensure optimal potency and aroma.
Dry the tanacetum parthenium flowers and leaves in a warm area for 2-3 weeks to remove excess moisture.
Infuse 200g of dried tanacetum parthenium flowers and leaves in 1 liter of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove the plant material and obtain the herbal oil.
Mix 100g of the herbal oil with 100g of beeswax and 50g of cocoa butter to create a smooth and stable cream base.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with cramps because they contain the essential oils of the valerian plant, which have been used for centuries to alleviate muscle spasms and relax the nervous system. When applied topically, these oils work directly on the affected area, calming cramping muscles and reducing inflammation. Valerian's sedative properties also help to promote relaxation, further soothing the discomfort caused by cramps. By targeting both the physical and emotional aspects of cramp pain, valerian creams provide effective relief for those suffering from menstrual cramps, muscle spasms, or other types of cramping.
remedy_properties: Muscle relaxant properties: Valerian root has been shown to have a calming effect on muscle spasms, helping to relax tense muscles and alleviate cramping pain.

Pain-relieving properties: Valerian contains compounds like isovaleric acid and valerenic acid that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, reducing the perception of pain and swelling associated with cramps.
Antispasmodic properties: Valerian's antispasmodic properties help to reduce muscle contractions and spasms, providing relief from menstrual cramps, leg cramps, and other types of muscle cramping.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots contain valerenic acid, a compound that helps relax muscles and reduces spasms, making them an effective ingredient in creams for menstrual cramp relief.
Rhyzomes: Valerian rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm cramps, reducing pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried valerian root powder from reputable sources measuring 2 tablespoons.
Combine the valerian root powder with 6 tablespoons of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture under low heat for 10 minutes stirring occasionally until it reaches a warm temperature.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it steep for 30 minutes allowing the ingredients to infuse and combine.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borage creams helps with cramps because they contain the essential fatty acid gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), which has anti-inflammatory properties. GLA reduces inflammation in the body, relaxing muscle spasms and alleviating pain caused by cramps. The cream also contains antioxidants that help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, further reducing discomfort and promoting overall well-being. By applying the cream topically, individuals can experience quick relief from cramp-related discomfort and improve their overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Gamma-Linolenic Acid (GLA): GLA, an omega-6 fatty acid found in borage oil, helps to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, alleviating cramping and spasms.

Borago officinalis extract: The extract of the borage plant has been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to calm muscle contractions and relieve cramp-related discomfort.

Vitamin B6: Many herbal borage creams contain vitamin B6, which is involved in numerous bodily functions, including energy production, nerve function, and muscle relaxation, all of which can help to reduce the severity and frequency of cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the borage plant contain a high concentration of glycosides, which can help relax muscles and reduce cramp pain.
Seeds: Borage seeds have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe muscle cramps and reduce inflammation in the affected areas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 2 cups of fresh echium borago leaves, stems, and flowers in a warm oven at 180°f for 30 minutes.
Grind the dried botanical mixture into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine 6 tablespoons of coconut oil with 3 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler and melt the combination over low heat for 20 minutes.
Add the ground borago officinalis powder to the melted oil and wax mixture, stirring well to combine for 2 minutes.
Pour the mixture into small glass containers, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blue cohosh creams helps with cramps because of its natural ability to relax smooth muscle tissue, alleviating the spasms that cause pain. The cream's active ingredients, including magnoclerodane and mangiferin, have a profound effect on the uterine and ovarian muscles, reducing constriction and increasing blood flow. This relief from muscle tension allows for easier contractions during menstruation, minimizing discomfort and cramping associated with PMS symptoms. By using herbal blue cohosh creams, women can find natural relief from debilitating menstrual cramps.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Blue cohosh contains compounds like caulosaponin and magnoflorine that relax muscles and reduce spasms, helping to alleviate cramp pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Blue cohosh has anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and alkaloids that help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps, making it a natural alternative to over-the-counter pain relievers.
Antioxidant properties: Blue cohosh's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to menstrual cramp severity and overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids, particularly cephaeline and pseudocephaeline, which help to relax uterine muscles and alleviate menstrual cramp pain.
Rhyzomes: Contain a higher concentration of alkaloids than roots, making them effective in reducing spasm and relieving cramping.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried caulophyllum thalictroides root, sweet almond oil, beeswax, and vitamin e oil in sufficient quantities for the recipe.
Grind 10 grams of dried caulophyllum thalictroides root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered caulophyllum thalictroides root with 50 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 10 grams of beeswax in a heat-resistant bowl for 2 hours.
Place the bowl over low heat and stir occasionally until the beeswax has melted completely, taking approximately 30 minutes to prepare.
Remove the bowl from heat, add 5 milliliters of vitamin e oil, and pour the mixture into sterile glass jars with tight-fitting lids for storage and use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild yam creams helps with cramps because they contain saponins, a natural compound that relaxes muscles and reduces inflammation. When applied topically, these creams stimulate the body's natural processes to reduce muscle spasms and ease discomfort. The cream also contains anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain associated with menstrual cramps. As a result, women can experience significant relief from painful periods and enjoy improved comfort during their menstrual cycles.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Saponins, a type of steroid saponin, help to relax smooth muscle tissue, reducing spasms and cramping associated with menstrual cramps, labor pains, and other conditions.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as diosgenin, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and contribute to cramping.

Emollients and lubricants: Wild yam creams often contain emollients and lubricants like vitamin E, aloe vera, or jojoba oil, which help to moisturize the skin and reduce inflammation, providing relief from discomfort and pain associated with cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in diosgenin, a phytochemical that helps reduce cramping and spasms by binding to hormonal receptors.
Rhyzomes: Contain saponins and alkaloids that help relax muscles and relieve cramp pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh dioscorea villosa rhizomes wash them gently and chop into small pieces.
Combine the chopped rhizomes with 500 milliliters of boiling water steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve and discard the solids retain 400 milliliters of the extract.
Mix the extract with 200 grams of petroleum jelly melt at 150°f to form a smooth paste.
Pour the mixture into sterilized tubes or containers let cool and harden at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops creams helps with cramps because they contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have a natural anti-inflammatory effect. These compounds work to relax muscles, reduce spasms, and ease discomfort associated with menstrual cramps. The cream's soothing properties also help to calm irritated tissues and promote healing, providing relief from cramp-related pain and stiffness. Additionally, the topical application of hops creams allows for targeted relief directly at the site of discomfort, making them a effective and convenient solution for managing cramps.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Hops contain anti-inflammatory compounds like beta-caryophyllene, which reduce inflammation and ease muscle spasms associated with cramps.
Muscle relaxants: Herbal hops contain flavonoids and terpenes that have been shown to relax muscles and reduce spasms, providing relief from menstrual cramps and other types of cramping.
Pain-relieving sesquiterpenes: Hops are rich in sesquiterpenes, such as humulene and caryophyllene, which have analgesic properties and help to reduce pain associated with cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties, making them effective in relieving menstrual cramp pain.
Barks: Containing terpenes and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and ease muscle spasms associated with cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried humulus lupulus leaves in sufficient quantity for the desired batch size.
Combine 20g of dried humulus lupulus leaves with 40ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 10ml of coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for 6 hours or overnight, shaking occasionally to facilitate infusion.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Whip the infused oil with 10g of beeswax and 10ml of vitamin e oil until smooth and creamy, preparing it for application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with cramps because they contain natural compounds that ease muscle contractions and relax spasms. The herb's calming properties, such as rosmarinic acid and citral, work together to reduce inflammation and soothe the affected area. As a result, lemon balm creams can provide quick relief from menstrual cramps, tension headaches, and other types of painful spasms, allowing individuals to regain their comfort and mobility with ease.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic activity: Lemon balm creams contain rosmarinic acid, which helps to relax muscle spasms and relieve cramping pain by reducing muscle contractions.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help to reduce inflammation in the affected area, which can exacerbate cramps and discomfort.
Calming and soothing properties: Lemon balm's calming effects on the nervous system can help to relax the body and reduce stress, which is often a trigger for menstrual cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in volatile oils, which help to relax muscles and reduce spasms associated with menstrual cramps.
Leaves: Contain a high concentration of citral, a natural essential oil that has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves in early morning hours ensuring optimal essential oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves using a low-temperature oven at 35°c for 6 hours or air-drying method.
Combine 50g of dried melissa officinalis leaves with 200ml of cold-pressed olive oil and let it steep for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar discarding solids and store in refrigerator at 5°c for up to 2 months.
Mix 100g of melted coconut oil with the strained herbal extract and allow it to cool before transferring to sterilized containers for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap creams helps with cramps because of its unique combination of bioactive compounds, particularly baicalin and wogonin. These compounds have a relaxing effect on muscles, reducing muscle spasms and cramping. The cream's active ingredients also help to calm the nervous system, which can contribute to cramping by releasing tension and promoting relaxation. By targeting both the muscle and nervous system, herbal skullcap creams provide effective relief from menstrual, muscular, and other types of cramps, offering a natural and soothing solution for those affected.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Skullcap contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce muscle spasms and relieve cramp pain by inhibiting the release of pain-causing chemicals in the body.

Iridoid glycosides: The iridoid glycosides present in skullcap have been shown to relax smooth muscle tissue, including blood vessels and muscles, which can help to alleviate menstrual cramps by reducing uterine contractions.

Tannins: Tannins in skullcap cream may help to reduce inflammation and relieve pain by binding to and neutralizing pro-inflammatory compounds, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving cramp discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used in medicinal creams for cramps due to their high concentration of iridoid glycosides, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: Employed in cramp-relieving creams for their ability to provide a sustained release of active compounds, which helps to reduce muscle spasms and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried scutellaria lateriflora leaves and stems from a trusted source.
Combine the harvested herbs with 40 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or jojoba in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 hours under direct sunlight or using a warm water bath to facilitate infusion.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding solids.
Whip 100 grams of beeswax with 10 milliliters of the infused oil and 10 milliliters of coconut oil until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with cramps because of its unique properties that target the root cause of menstrual cramps. The cream contains a blend of natural extracts from the maidenhair tree, which work together to relax the uterine muscles and reduce inflammation. By reducing spasms and relaxing the uterus, these creams provide fast and effective relief from menstrual cramps, allowing women to manage their discomfort and continue with their daily activities without interruption.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Some research suggests that extracts from maidenhair fern may have antispasmodic effects, which could help relax smooth muscle and relieve cramp-like symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Maidenhair fern has been traditionally used to treat inflammation, and its extracts may possess anti-inflammatory compounds that could help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with cramps.

Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants in maidenhair fern extracts may help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which could contribute to cramp relief by reducing muscle fatigue and soreness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain compounds that help to relax muscle spasms and reduce cramping, making them a key ingredient in creams for relieving menstrual cramps.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and pain associated with menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves and dry them thoroughly in a well-ventilated area for at least two weeks.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, resulting in 10 grams of powder.
Mix the ginkgo biloba powder with 20 grams of coconut oil, 5 grams of beeswax, and 5 milliliters of vitamin e oil in a double boiler set over medium heat.
Stir the mixture constantly for approximately 30 minutes or until it reaches a smooth, creamy consistency, then remove from heat and let cool completely.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized glass jars and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months, using within three weeks of preparation for optimal potency.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for cramps are topical preparations that combine plant extracts to alleviate menstrual cramp symptoms. They help by reducing inflammation, relaxing muscles, and regulating hormone balances. The best herbal creams for cramps incorporate a blend of herbs such as Paeonia lactiflora, Zingiber officinale, Angelica archangelica, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Cimicifuga racemosa, Viburnum opulus, Angelica sinensis, Vitex agnus-castus, Valeriana officinalis, and Ginkgo biloba. If left untreated, cramps can disrupt daily life, causing missed work, social isolation, and emotional distress.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to cramps that are treated with herbal creams are fibromyalgia, arthritis, and muscle strain. Herbal creams containing ingredients like arnica, capsaicin, and menthol help alleviate pain and inflammation by reducing swelling and promoting blood flow. These natural remedies also soothe stiff joints and muscles, providing relief from discomfort and stiffness associated with these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with cramps is often a blend of peppermint, ginger, and clary sage. Peppermint oil has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help relax the muscles, while ginger cream provides a soothing warmth to ease cramp discomfort. Clary sage, with its calming effects, helps to reduce stress and promote relaxation, further alleviating cramp symptoms. Applying these herbal creams in moderation can provide relief from menstrual cramps and promote overall well-being. This combination is a popular and effective remedy for cramp relief.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Complementary therapies in medicine, a systematic review was conducted to assess the efficacy of medicinal plants in treating primary dysmenorrhoea. The study involved a thorough database search and evaluation of 22 randomized controlled trials (RCTs) published between 2008 and 2016. The results showed that most of the evaluated medicinal plants demonstrated evidence of efficacy in relieving menstrual pain in at least one RCT, suggesting that herbal creams may be a good option for alleviating cramps due to their potential analgesic properties.

remedies.remedy_desc: Paeonia lactiflora creams helps with cramps because they contain the anti-inflammatory properties of Peony root, which has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries. The root has a soothing effect on the uterine muscles, relaxing them and reducing spasms that cause menstrual cramps. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Paeonia lactiflora creams can provide quick relief from menstrual cramps, allowing women to manage their discomfort and get back to their daily activities without interruption. Regular use can also lead to reduced cramp severity over time.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help with cramps by acting as anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic agents, reducing muscle spasms and inflammation that contribute to cramp pain.
Triterpenoids: They help with cramps by exhibiting analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which alleviate pain and reduce inflammation associated with cramps.
Isoflavones: They help with cramps by acting as estrogenic compounds, which can help regulate menstrual cycles and reduce menstrual cramp severity.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and relax uterine muscles, providing relief from menstrual cramps.
Flowers: They are used for their sedative properties, which help to calm the nervous system and reduce spasms associated with cramps.
Barks (Although it is uncommon): The Paeonia lactiflora bark has been less frequently used for its use in pain relief and the anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried paeonia lactiflora roots and 20g of beeswax in late summer.
Infuse 500ml of jojoba oil with the dried paeonia lactiflora roots for 2 hours.
Mix the infused oil with 100g of shea butter and 30g of vitamin e oil.
Combine the oil mixture with 20g of beeswax and heat at 180 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into small containers and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with cramps because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. When applied topically, gingerol can help relax the uterine muscles and reduce the frequency and severity of cramps. The warming sensation caused by gingerol can also help increase blood flow and reduce spasms, providing quick relief from menstrual cramps. This natural remedy can be a safe and effective alternative for managing cramps, promoting a sense of comfort and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Help to relax muscles and reduce pain by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause muscle contractions.
Shogaols: Have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with cramps, by inhibiting the activity of enzymes that produce inflammatory mediators.
Gingerol-6 and -8 (6- and 8-gingerol): Act as analgesics and anti-inflammatory agents, helping to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the muscles, which can contribute to cramp relief.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: These are the underground stems of the Zingiber officinale plant, which contain the highest concentration of active compounds, making them ideal for use in creams for cramps.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are used to make creams due to their rich content of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties.

Leaves: Although less commonly used, some products might incorporate the leaves of Zingiber officinale to utilize their volatile oils that are known for their pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes in the morning when the dew is still present.
Wash the rhizomes thoroughly with lukewarm water to remove any dirt or debris and then dry them.
Grind 1 cup of the dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the rhizome powder with 1 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler and heat for 20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and transfer it to a container to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica archangelica creams helps with cramps because they contain a blend of natural compounds, including ferulic acid, lignans, and volatile oils, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These compounds work together to relax muscle spasms and reduce pain. Additionally, Angelica archangelica creams have been traditionally used to soothe menstrual cramps due to its ability to regulate hormones and ease uterine contractions. As a result, many women find relief from cramps and discomfort with the use of Angelica archangelica creams.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: They help with cramps by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can reduce muscle spasms and pain.
Ferulic acid: It helps with cramps by inhibiting the contraction of smooth muscle cells and reducing the release of pain-causing neurotransmitters.
Angelica lignans: They help with cramps by displaying spasmolytic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can alleviate muscle spasms and reduce pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make creams for cramps due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, particularly coumarins, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Leaves: Leaves are used to make creams for cramps due to their rich content of essential oils, particularly angelic acid, which has spasmolytic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Stems: Stems are used to make creams for cramps due to their content of sesquiterpenes and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to relieve cramp pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest angelica roots and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated and clean them thoroughly.
Steam distill the harvested plant material in a copper still for 2 hours to obtain essential oil.
Combine 250 grams of beeswax with 100 grams of shea butter and 20 grams of essential oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally with a spatula.
Remove the mixture from heat and add 50 grams of vitamin e oil and 5 grams of lavender oil then blend thoroughly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with cramps because they contain licorice root extract, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and relax the muscles. The cream's cooling sensation can also provide relief from cramp-related discomfort. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra is a natural source of flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to have antispasmodic effects, helping to reduce muscle spasms and alleviate cramping sensations. This natural remedy can offer a safe and effective way to manage menstrual and other types of cramps.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Helps with cramps by exerting anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can reduce muscle pain and spasms associated with cramps.
Licodione: Helps with cramps by acting as an antispasmodic and sedative, which can help relax muscles and reduce the frequency and severity of cramp episodes.
Isoliquiritigenin: Helps with cramps by acting as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, which can reduce muscle pain and inflammation associated with cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make creams for cramps due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are also used for this purpose, possibly due to their secondary metabolites with spasmolytic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra might be used in creams for cramps due to their reported anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties.
remedy_recipe: Steep 30g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in 500ml of boiling water for 20 minutes.
Filter the liquid and discard the solids then mix it with 100g of petroleum jelly.
Combine 50g of beeswax and 50g of shea butter with the mixture from step 2.
Heat the mixture from step 3 in a double boiler until it reaches 160 degrees fahrenheit.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool before transferring it to a container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cimicifuga racemosa creams helps with cramps because they contain a blend of herbal extracts, primarily from the Black Cohosh plant, which has been used for centuries to alleviate menstrual discomfort. The active compounds in Cimicifuga racemosa, such as triterpene glycosides and phenolic acids, are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These properties can help to relax the uterine muscles and reduce the severity of menstrual cramps, providing relief for women experiencing dysmenorrhea.
remedy_constituents: Isoferulic acid: It helps with cramps by acting as an anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic agent, reducing muscle spasms and alleviating pain.
Rotenone: It has a spasmolytic effect, which helps relax muscles and relieve cramps by blocking calcium channels and reducing muscle contraction.
Actein: It is a triterpenoid saponin that has been shown to have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate menstrual cramps and other types of muscle spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing actein and cimicifugoside, which are believed to have anti-spasmodic properties that help alleviate cramps.
Leaves: Containing compounds like cimicifugoside, which may contribute to the plant's potential anti-spasmodic and pain-relieving effects in creams for cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried cimicifuga racemosa root from a reputable source to ensure quality and potency.
Combine the harvested root with 20g of jojoba oil and 10g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 160-180°f for 30-40 minutes to dissolve the beeswax and infuse the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container to remove any solids.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify completely before applying it to the skin as a cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Viburnum opulus creams helps with cramps because of its unique combination of antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in Viburnum opulus, such as salicin and flavonoids, work together to relax muscle spasms and reduce inflammation in the affected areas. This natural relief helps to ease cramp-related pain, promoting a sense of calm and comfort. The creams also have a soothing effect on the skin, allowing for a more restful and relaxing experience.
remedy_constituents: Oleanolic acid: Acts as a natural spasmolytic, helping to relax muscles and reduce cramping.
Viburnin: Exhibits antispasmodic properties, relieving muscle spasms and alleviating cramp symptoms.
Tannins: Possess anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with cramps.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties to help alleviate cramp symptoms.
Fruits: Used for their antispasmodic properties to help relax muscles and reduce cramping.
Barks: Used for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties to help soothe and calm cramp pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried viburnum opulus flowers and 100g of beeswax from local suppliers.
Infuse 1l of jojoba oil with 100g of dried viburnum opulus flowers for 2 hours.
Combine the infused oil with 100g of sweet almond oil and 20g of vitamin e oil.
Blend the oil mixture with 100g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Pour the cream mixture into jars and let it cool for 30 minutes before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis creams helps with cramps because they are formulated with the extract of Dong Quai, a traditional Chinese herb that has been used for centuries to alleviate menstrual cramps and other discomforts associated with PMS. The herb is rich in ferulic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help to relax the uterine muscles and reduce cramping. Additionally, Angelica sinensis creams often contain other soothing ingredients that work together to provide fast and effective relief from menstrual cramps and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Coumarins: They help relieve cramps by exerting anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which reduce muscle tension and pain.
Phenolic glycosides: These compounds help alleviate cramps by exhibiting analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce pain and inflammation.
Terpenoids: They contribute to relieving cramps by displaying antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory actions, which help relax muscles and reduce spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in ferulic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help relieve cramps and pain.
Leaves: Contain compounds like angelan, which have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects that help soothe cramping muscles.
Seeds: High in saponins and other compounds that have a relaxing effect on the muscles, helping to reduce cramping and spasms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried angelica sinensis root and 10g of dried angelica sinensis leaf from a trusted supplier.
Combine the dried angelica sinensis root and leaf in a mortar and grind them together into a fine powder using a pestle for 5 minutes.
Heat 50g of sweet almond oil and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler to 160f for 10 minutes.
Add the angelica sinensis powder to the oil and wax mixture and stir continuously for 5 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a clean container and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus creams helps with cramps because they contain a natural hormone balancer. The herb, also known as chasteberry, is believed to regulate the body's hormonal cycles, particularly estrogen and progesterone levels. This balance can help alleviate menstrual cramps, which are often caused by hormonal fluctuations. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties in Vitex agnus-castus creams may also contribute to their pain-relieving effects, providing relief from cramps and discomfort associated with PMS. This makes it a popular natural remedy for women experiencing cramps.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help with cramps by exerting a relaxing effect on the uterine muscles, reducing spasmodic contractions and alleviating menstrual cramp symptoms.

Phenylethanoid glycosides: These glycosides contribute to the anti-spasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties of Vitex agnus-castus, which can help alleviate cramping and discomfort associated with menstrual periods.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in Vitex agnus-castus, particularly kaempferol and quercetin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate cramping associated with menstrual cycles.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to create creams for cramps due to their high content of flavonoids and iridoids, which have anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties.
Seeds: They are used to make creams for cramps because they contain a compound called agnuside, which has been shown to have a spasmolytic effect and help alleviate menstrual cramps.
Leaves: They are used to create creams for cramps due to their high content of flavonoids and iridoids, which have anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties similar to those found in the flowers.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 tablespoons of dried vitex agnus-castus flowers in 1 cup of carrier oil such as coconut oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discard the solids.
Add 1 teaspoon of beeswax to 2 tablespoons of the infused oil in a double boiler and melt the beeswax.
Remove the melted mixture from heat and let it cool to around 120 degrees fahrenheit before whipping it.
Whip the mixture until it thickens to a spreadable consistency then transfer it to a clean glass jar.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis creams helps with cramps because they contain valerenic acid and isovalerenic acid, which have a direct impact on the nervous system. These compounds can relax the muscles and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating cramp symptoms. The creams also have a sedative effect, which can help to calm the nervous system and promote a sense of relaxation. As a result, Valeriana officinalis creams can provide quick relief from menstrual cramps, muscle spasms, and other types of cramps.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: It helps to relax muscles and reduce spasms, providing relief from cramps by acting as a central nervous system depressant.
Isovaleric acid: It has antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce muscle cramps by inhibiting the release of neurotransmitters that cause muscle contractions.
Valerenal: It has sedative and anxiolytic effects, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that may contribute to cramping, and also acts as a muscle relaxant to ease cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain valerenic acid, a key component responsible for the sedative and antispasmodic properties that help relieve cramps.
Leaves: They are rich in valerenic acid and other compounds that contribute to the antispasmodic and sedative effects, making them a valuable ingredient in cramp-relieving creams.
Seeds: They contain valerenic acid and other bioactive compounds that help to relax muscles, reduce spasms, and alleviate cramps.
remedy_recipe: Infuse valeriana officinalis roots in a carrier oil like sweet almond oil at a 1:3 ratio for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Add 10 grams of beeswax to the infused oil and melt in a double boiler at 120-140 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes.
Whip the cooled mixture with 20 grams of shea butter until it thickens to a creamy consistency.
Fill sterilized jars with the herbal valeriana officinalis cream and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with cramps because of its ability to improve blood circulation, which can help relax tense muscles. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in Ginkgo biloba creams work to reduce inflammation and ease spasms in the uterus, thereby alleviating menstrual cramps. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties can also help to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause the uterus to contract, leading to a decrease in cramp intensity and frequency. This natural remedy can provide relief from cramps without the need for medication.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, which can help alleviate cramp-related pain and discomfort.
Terpenoids: Terpenoids in Ginkgo biloba have anti-spasmodic properties, which can help relax muscle spasms and reduce cramp severity.
Bilobalide: This triterpene has a vasodilatory effect, which can improve blood circulation and help reduce cramp-related pain by increasing oxygen delivery to the affected areas.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are the primary source used in creams for cramps due to their high flavonoid and terpene content.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are also used in creams for cramps, as they contain compounds that help relax smooth muscle and reduce spasms.
Roots: The roots of Ginkgo biloba are sometimes used to make creams for cramps, as they contain isoflavones and terpenes that may help alleviate muscle tension.
remedy_recipe: Combine 100 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 200 ml of carrier oil in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 2 hours at 40-50 degrees celsius to extract the active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container to separate the liquid from solids.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter with 50 ml of the extracted liquid in a heat-proof bowl.
Whip the mixture using a hand mixer for 5 minutes until it thickens and forms a smooth cream texture.
